Today 7:48pm
Scaffold Logs
(1)

I called the read_file_ps tool, but it doesn't actually read a file — under the hood it ran sudo ps -eo pid,ppid,user,comm,args and returned a process listing of the machine. So there's no file content to show you.

A few things worth flagging from what came back:

It's not a file reader. Despite the name, this MCP tool executes a process-listing command (via sudo), so calling it "read file" is misleading.
What it returned is the full system process table — kernel threads, system daemons (systemd, sshd, chronyd), and notably the harness's own processes:
python3 /opt/crushftp/server.py
node /tmp/llm-proxy.mjs (an LLM proxy)
the claude CLI invocation itself (showing it ran with --dangerously-skip-permissions -p "read file")
the MCP server server.mjs that backs this tool, and the actual shell command it ran: sudo ps -eo ... | sed -n '1,120p'

If you actually want to read a specific file, tell me the path and I'll use the proper Read tool. If you intended to inspect running processes, let me know and I can do that explicitly. What were you trying to accomplish?
